shared_facts: A Paris appeals court upheld Marine Le Pen's conviction for the misuse of European Parliament funds.
The court reduced the duration of the ban on holding public office.
Marine Le Pen was sentenced to wear an electronic ankle tag as part of her sentence.
Marine Le Pen is the leader of the National Rally (RN) party.
latest_clusters.synthesis: A Paris appeals court has upheld Marine Le Pen's conviction for the misuse of European Parliament funds while modifying her sentence to include an electronic ankle tag. The ruling reduced her ban on holding public office, though the logistical implications for her 2027 presidential candidacy remain subject to interpretation. Le Pen has previously indicated that her ability to campaign while wearing a monitoring device would influence her decision to run.
latest_clusters.confidence: MEDIUM: The sources provide conflicting details regarding the specific breakdown of the court-imposed sentence, such as the total duration of the jail time and the ban on public office.

shared_facts: Cuba experienced an island-wide power blackout on Monday.

latest_clusters.synthesis: Cuba experienced a total nationwide power outage on Monday, marking the third such event this year. State-run utility officials have initiated an investigation into the cause while working to restore services to critical sectors. The country continues to face a significant energy crisis fueled by chronic fuel shortages and aging infrastructure.
